title: 6 Navigating Enterprise Fraud Management
body_text:
6	Navigating Enterprise Fraud Management
6.1	Overview of Navigating Enterprise Fraud Management
The Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) interface has three common features: the page header, the page sidebar, and the page body. The page header is used to navigate between different sections, the page sidebar is used to navigate within a section, and the page body contains most of the important information for that section. The dashboard is the first page you see when you log in. See Figure 1.
Note:
Training videos
are available for the procedures in this chapter.
ГўВ‘
Incidents
ГўВҖВ“ Select this link to access the list of incidents that require your attention.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Events
ГўВҖВ“ Select this link to search for risk scores, event types, and event times.
ГўВ‘Вў
Reports
ГўВҖВ“ Select this link to access EFM generated reports.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Settings
ГўВҖВ“ Select this link to create and modify incident lists, teams, and watchlists (e.g.,В fraudulent emails).
ГўВ‘ВӨ
System
ГўВҖВ“ Select this link to view an audit log of user activity.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Search box
ГўВҖВ“ Use the search box to search for events processed by the system and find events or entities that match your search criteria.
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Documentation
ГўВҖВ“ Select this icon to open a sidebar with information related to the page you are viewing.
ГўВ‘В§
Current User
ГўВҖВ“ Select this icon, then select your username to open your user profile page or select
Logout
to sign out of the system.
ГўВ‘ВЁ
Messages field
ГўВҖВ“ Send messages to other users or teams in your organization. This messaging feature can only be used to communicate with other EFM users within your organization.
Figure 1: Fraud Alert Services Dashboard in EFM
Related Information
Training Videos:
Fraud Alert Services in EFM
6.2	Changing Your Display Name
You can update preferences on your profile page. To access your profile page:
Select the Current User icon, then select your username. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Access to Preferences
In the Preferences section, you can make changes. See Figure 3.
ГўВ‘
Locale
ГўВҖВ“ The system default is English (United States). The other option is English (United Kingdom).
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Time Format
ГўВҖВ“ The recommended setting is ГўВҖВң24 hourГўВҖВқ as this format syncs with the format used in the Online Administrative System (OAS).
ГўВ‘Вў
Display Name
ГўВҖВ“ The default is your username. To change it, enter the name you want displayed on the system.
Select
Update
to save your changes.
Figure 3: Update Preferences
Related Information
Training Videos:
Fraud Alert Services in EFM
6.3	Using the Messages Function
The messages bar is visible on all pages and indicates how many messages you have. This feature can only be used to communicate with other EFM users within your organization. To send a message:
Select
New
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Create a New Message
Make selections for the message. See Figure 5.
ГўВ‘
Message Type
ГўВҖВ“ Select
Normal
or
Important
.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Send to
ГўВҖВ“ Select who the message is for; your options are: Everyone, Users(s), Team(s), Role(s). This messaging feature can only be used to communicate with other EFM users in your organization.
ГўВ‘Вў
Start typing
ГўВҖВ“ The name of the user, team, or role. This field doesnГўВҖВҷt appear if you selected ГўВҖВңEveryoneГўВҖВқ.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Subject
ГўВҖВ“ This field is optional.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Message
ГўВҖВ“ Type your message.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Send message
ГўВҖВ“ Select to send the message.
Figure 5: Send a Message
Your inbox displays the number of unread messages. See Figure 6.
ГўВ‘
Select a message to read the contents.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the dash to minimize the messages window.
ГўВ‘Вў
Select
Acknowledge
to remove a message after you have read it.
Figure 6: Reading and Acknowledging Messages
END
